sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the provided transcript:
Subjective:
The patient, a 38-year-old male, presented with a complaint of shortness of breath, which he has been experiencing for an unknown duration.
He reports that his breathing is not what it used to be and he catches his breath a lot more than he used to, considering himself to be pretty healthy.
He mentions that the shortness of breath is sudden and unexplained.
He also reports eye watering, which he attributes to allergies, similar to his dog.
He denies any other symptoms such as rash, nausea, vomiting, lip or throat swelling, or diarrhea.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of visiting urgent care and being prescribed an inhaler for asthma symptoms, which he currently uses.
Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing shortness of breath in various situations, including at home, outside, and near his cat.
Current medications: I prescribed Albuterol inhaler as needed for asthma symptoms.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's pulse is okay, and his pulse ox is normal.
He does not appear to be in any distress.
Physical exam: The patient does not have a rash on his body, no angioedema, and no audible stridor.
I appreciate faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
Test Results: The patient had a normal chest X-ray and pulmonary function test.
Assessment / Problem List:
I diagnose the patient with newly diagnosed allergic asthma.
Problem list: Asthma; Active.
Plan:
I want the patient to continue using the Albuterol inhaler as soon as symptoms start to flare up.
I prescribed Singulair 10mg once daily to help decrease the occurrences of asthma.
I will proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing to identify potential triggers.
We will schedule the skin testing for next week.
The patient is to return to the office to schedule the skin testing.
Follow-up:
The patient is to return to the office next week to schedule the skin testing.
